constraints: Do not store all values; only maintain running aggregates.
disallowed_assumptions: At least one value is added before current() is called.
expected_failure_modes: Storing all values instead of running aggregates.
Returning 0.0 or None for min/max when empty (wrong).
prohibited_claims: Model superiority — not authorized by this task.
Wrapper improvement without paired comparison — not measured.
prompt:
Implement a class `StreamingAggregator`:
- `__init__(self)` — initializes counters to zero.
- `add(self, value: float) -> None` — add a single value to the aggregate.
- `current(self) -> dict` — return `{'count': int, 'sum': float, 'mean': float, 'min': float | None, 'max': float | None}` based on all values seen so far.

The aggregator must process values one at a time without storing the entire dataset.
It should compute running statistics in O(1) memory per operation.

Handle:
- No values added yet: count=0, sum=0.0, mean=0.0, min=None, max=None.
- Single value: min and max equal the value.
- Negative values.
- Adding None (skip it, do not count).
title: Streaming-Friendly Aggregation
